text: Tea Lady's dual mode — treat as execution-survival role (requiring proc-confounder) or as night-protection role (no counterpart requirement)? v2.1 recommends execution-survival treatment, but designers may disagree.

Lleech as a partial execution-survival counterpart — scripts with Pacifist/Sailor/Fool/Tea Lady and Lleech as Demon but no Devil's Advocate: is Lleech satisfying the proc-confounder requirement? v2.1 treats it as partial (warning, not error).

When does Boffin-as-X create plausible bluff space? Boffin covers confirmations only when the role is on script, not in play, and not excluded by a jinx. Whether this satisfies a proc-confounder requirement depends on bag-arithmetic.

Slayer with Boffin as the only proc-confounder — Boffin-only Slayer cover is partial: Boffin only matters when Slayer is not in play. A Slayer+Boffin-only script has no cover for the 'Slayer in play and shoots Demon' scenario.

Mayor's bounce-confounders versus claim-confounders — Lunatic and Magician muddy kill patterns (bounce-confounders) but do not undermine the Mayor claim itself. They should not satisfy the claim-confounder hard-error lint alone.

Cannibal-as-Professor — Cannibal eating a dead Professor produces a Professor confirmation signature. This is a proc-confounder for Professor confirmations, but good-aligned (good-on-good doubt, not evil bluff). Surface for designer awareness.

Gambler + Evil Twin — a Gambler can guess a Traveller (never a script character, so a guaranteed-wrong guess and certain death) to deliberately die and thereby prove they held the real Gambler ability. This certain-death-on-demand lets a genuine Gambler confirm their identity at will, eroding the identity/bluff uncertainty the Evil Twin pair relies on (evil loses a clean way to contest a Gambler claim). Surfaces on Sugar & Spice. Consider a jinx/ruling, or escalating to a warning.
